name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: My Faith Votes Board Member Honored
body:
Johnnie Moore Receives Medal of Valor

View this email in your browser

Dear Peter,

On April 5, 2017, My Faith Votes' Board
Member, Johnnie
Moore, was awarded the prestigious 'Medal of
Valor' by the Simon Wiesenthal Center.

The Medal was awarded in recognition of Mr.
Moore's advocacy for persecuted Christians and his role in
rescuing thousands from ISIS affected territories. Mr. Moore
accepted the award on behalf of the pastors, priests, and
multitudes of Middle Eastern Christians who live under constant
threat because of their faith. He also gave special recognition
to courageous Jews and Muslims who defended and aided
Christians-especially the Simon Wiesenthal Center, who first
declared that what was happening to Christians in Iraq and Syria
was in fact genocide, and King Abdullah of Jordan, who has
allowed his country to be a place of refuge for persecuted
Christians and religious minorities in the Middle East.

"I've just done what I could," Moore said in
his acceptance remarks. "No one person can save the world, but we
can all save a life. We can provide to the world that the best of
faith is still stronger than the worst of religion."

At My Faith Votes, our call to do just this;
to act on our faith, because it has something essential to offer
to the world around us. We hold in the highest regard Mr. Moore's
devotion to religious freedom for all and the sanctity & dignity
of human life, and we are honored to have him server as a board
member of My Faith Votes.

To learn more about Mr. Moore and the award
please visit MyFaithVotes.org/news.

Faith makes America stronger,

Jason Yates
Chief Executive Officer
My Faith Votes

My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement
focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in
local and national elections and be active members of society. By
partnering with local churches, pastors and national faith
leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and resources Christians to
lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and
politics.

**WHITE HOUSE MEMO**
President Donald J. Trump calls on all civilized nations to join us in seeking to end the slaughter and bloodshed in Syria – and also to end terrorism of all kinds and all types. We ask for God’s wisdom as we face the challenges of our very troubled world. We pray for the lives of the wounded, and for the souls of those who have passed. And we hope that as long as America stands for justice, then peace will – in the end – prevail.

name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: Congratulations, Supreme Court Justice Neil M. Gorsuch!
body:
Your faithful prayers for his confirmation made such a
difference. Thank you!

View this email in your browser

Dear Peter,

Once again, the united faith and action of
millions in the My Faith Votes community demonstrated the power
of prayer and making your voice heard with your Senators.

The confirmation of Justice Gorsuch secured a
significant victory, as we fight to restore biblical values to
the heart of our great nation.

Please continue to support Justice Gorsuch,
and pray that he would champion constitutional law by
faithfully…

Protecting religious freedom for all
Protecting the sanctity and dignity of
human life

Promoting strong families and marriages

Promoting personal responsibility and
compassion for those in need

Again, thank you for
praying!

With gratitude,
Jason Yates
Chief Executive Officer
My Faith Votes

My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement
focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in
local and national elections and be active members of society. By
partnering with local churches, pastors and national faith
leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and resources Christians to
lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and
politics.

name: Team Kaine
subject: "nuclear option"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

Last week, Tim wrote to tell you he'd be opposing Judge Neil Gorsuch for the Supreme Court.

Since then, Senate Republicans have voted to exercise the so-called "nuclear option" in order to eliminate the Senate filibuster for Supreme Court nominees and bypass the Senators who oppose Judge Neil Gorsuch's confirmation to the Supreme Court.

**Derek, if you 've had it with right-wing politicians who put partisan politicians over long-term progress for our nation, [we hope you'll chip in to support Tim's reelection.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=xX4p4nNufY8bwumxkeo2u3Nvnog4wHTJ)**

As a result of Republicans' rule change, we're faced with a Supreme Court justice who is expected to spend the rest of his lifetime appointment ruling against workers, women, and children.

Tim took a stand for what was right -- and **now the GOP will be working extra hard to defeat him in 2018**. They're already raising money and deciding which extreme candidate will run as his opponent.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Zero to five
body:
#
Friend --
Next month our twins, Caroline and Jackson, will turn three years old, and our third baby Gillum is due to make his first appearance into the world as well. And I can't help but think about how different things are for them than they were for me and the kids in my neighborhood growing up.
Studies say that the difference between kids who grow up like Caroline and Jackson, in middle class families like ours, or even more affluent ones, will have heard about 30 million more words in the first three years of their life than a kid who grows up in a low-income family in my childhood neighborhood of Richmond Heights. That's not because any parent loves their child more than another -- we all want the best for our kids. But when low-income parents have to work multiple jobs to make ends meet, sometimes those extra moments - like reading with their kids before bed - are simply not on the schedule.
But that's something we know we can change, through providing quality public education earlier. [**If you agree that we need to close the 30 million word gap and provide public education earlier for Florida kids, add your name to our petition right now.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33b3/9e00690/499b14de/24034674/2407244491/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/ece?source=em20170407-ece-b")
Right now, we begin most of our public education at five years old -- but research tells us that 90% of a child's brain development happens from ages zero to five. When I'm Governor, I'll work to provide education earlier for Florida kids, so that we can take advantage of this crucial time in their lives, and ensure that every student is kindergarten ready in Florida.
We cannot continue to force our kids to scramble to catch up to their peers in kindergarten -- we need to make sure all of our kids have the access to the resources they need to fulfill their learning potential.
[**Will you join us in these efforts by adding your name to our petition in support of early childhood education in Florida now?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33b3/9e00690/499b14de/24034674/2407244491/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/ece?source=em20170407-ece-b")
Bring it home,
Andrew

**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33b3/9e00690/499b14de/24034677/2407244491/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-april2017?refcode=em20170407-ece")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33b3/9e00690/499b14de/24034676/2407244491/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). **_Donald Jr. Is Coming to Town_**
By now you've seen the big news that Donald Trump Jr. is coming back to the Hoosier State to headline our 2017 Spring Dinner. Donald understands the lessons of 2016 better than anyone else having played a critical role in the election of our nation’s 45th president of the United States. Having him visit Indiana in a non-election year is a testament to the importance our state will have next year as we work to defeat Joe Donnelly, protect our Congressional delegation and maintain our hold of statewide offices and supermajorities in the State House and Senate. For more information about sponsorships and tickets, [click here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5d7776374a&e=1acd9bb3d6).

\- Kyle
(Photo: Chairman Hupfer had the opportunity to present to the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series 2016-2017 Class Friday morning. Don't forget their Annual Luncheon with Seema Verma coming up on April 21. **NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, APRIL 7th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
It's official! Neil Gorsuch is our newest Supreme Court Justice. Try as they might, disgruntled obstructionist Senators Shaheen and Hassan could not stop this highly qualified nominee. They continue to be out of touch and out of line.
It was interesting to read in [_**today’s "USA TODAY"**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyH0T0-2By576-2FBQcuGTCZ-2Ftp8ePBMJ0Nt5d-2BWgN2-2BjlrzFWitIeL5lGOZKs-2FjPD2j7OEz0kxaHblnJorviEYgq-2BiOjFev0xgVdkJ18BRcsh-2BLZNxpR-2FKB0gpPiRRtyTgeutCG-2FfDtq-2BGlQ8sb8dN9fuPWxJyiHspPt58JVU-2FFHjwX8nCvJypkTuJOwjBkdq7XOeg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MHyt1Gy2oJ3Br1BMyYQDkqJb6h6BdH0nTr8vWXC79fG8cxmiHgWLDHzn1QEI7PYPso-2FADrZmRPXmNoooOosFpvZfF9w4qL73pn87m-2BnPXlioFbe5JR9lEh8PaBiypmtBNQXUbSpuLDpBKP06g-2BQKZ6fK1DGRbqvC0EgDYY4dg86WoO7wrg1u-2F8ti5keMSfXBeV7StQg2mOX-2BM02VvpU5-2BVQ7jexisqDqqK4GT-2BSxpzrrWvtg10cFX6e4gQGf0kbDW-2F99diiKIOyiZsphZvjwD0w-3D-3D) that a University of Massachusetts political science professor who writes on judicial nominations blames Democrats as instigators and Republicans as annihilators. He said that “Republicans are master fighters...they do payback like we’ve never seen before.” Makes me proud to be a Republican!
Let’s keep that fighting spirit up and make sure we all work together to win the upcoming Special Elections, in the House and Senate.
We've got some great candidates and they need our help to win. | _**Senate Republicans**_
**Birdsell, Bradley praise solution to town election ratification process** ****
Concord, NH -Today, the Senate passed a measure to ratify town elections moved due to a March 14, 2017 winter storm.
Senator Regina Birdsell (R-Hampstead), chair of Election Law and Internal Affairs, and Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statements:
“I am pleased that the Senate passed a workable solution that would allow those towns who moved their town meeting day elections due to the storm on March 14, 2017 to properly ratify the results and adopt bond measures ahead of critical local bonding deadlines,“ said Senator Regina Birdsell (R-Hampstead).
“This solution came by working with communities to address the specific challenges faced by moving an election and will allow results to take effect following a public hearing and a vote of the governing body. This balances the need for towns to quickly ratify election results while giving the voters a voice in the solution through a public meeting,” added Birdsell.
“By introducing legislation that narrowly allows for towns to ratify elections moved due to the March 14, 2017 storm,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro).
“Locally elected officials will now have a path forward to legally implement municipal bonds. I look forward to working with the legislature and the Governor to create a system to make sure the confusion the resulted from the storm does not happen again.”

Neil Gorsuch is our newest Supreme Court Justice. Try as they might, disgruntled obstructionist Senators Shaheen and Hassan could not stop this highly qualified nominee. They continue to be out of touch and out of line. It was interesting to read in that a University of Massachusetts political science professor who writes on judicial nominations blames Democrats as instigators and Republicans as annihilators. He said that “Republicans are master fighters...they do payback like we’ve never seen before.” Makes me proud to be a Republican! Let’s keep that fighting spirit up and make sure we all work together to win the upcoming Special Elections, in the House and Senate. We've got some great candidates and they need our help to win. To that end, I hope you’ll join our to sharpen your communications skills and learn how to use social media like a pro. We know the Democrats are hurting—they’re angry and probably should receive some kind of award for being the “Biggest Loser.” Let’s seize the moment and keep them in “loser” status! Senator Regina Birdsell (R-Hampstead), chair of Election Law and Internal Affairs, and Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statements: “I am pleased that the Senate passed a workable solution that would allow those towns who moved their town meeting day elections due to the storm on March 14, 2017 to properly ratify the results and adopt bond measures ahead of critical local bonding deadlines,“ said Senator Regina Birdsell (R-Hampstead). “This solution came by working with communities to address the specific challenges faced by moving an election and will allow results to take effect following a public hearing and a vote of the governing body. This balances the need for towns to quickly ratify election results while giving the voters a voice in the solution through a public meeting,” added Birdsell. “By introducing legislation that narrowly allows for towns to ratify elections moved due to the March 14, 2017 storm,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro). “Locally elected officials will now have a path forward to legally implement municipal bonds. body:
Under normal Senate rules, Judge Gorsuch's appointment to the Supreme Court should have ended when Democrats had enough votes to filibuster his confirmation.
---
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gorsuch-loss?refcode=DPE-20170407-TurningPointDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Friend --

Senate Democrats filibustered Judge Gorsuch's appointment to the Supreme Court. Under normal Senate rules, his nomination should have ended then and there.

But instead, Republicans decided that the rules just didn't apply to them and demolished the filibuster -- breaking decades of Senate rules -- rather than admit defeat.

We have to hold the Republicans accountable for destroying the Senate rules and ramming through Donald Trump's right-wing nominee -- and the way we'll do it is by electing Democrats in 2018.

**[This is a critical moment to strike back, Team. Chip in $1 to show that you're standing with Senate Democrats right now.](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/gorsuch-loss?refcode=DPE-20170407-TurningPointDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**

Our Democratic senators fought hard to demand a nominee who would protect our rights, rather than defend corporations and billionaires.

They asked the tough questions in hearings, exposing Judge Gorsuch's troubling record on access to women's health care, workers' rights and more.

They took to the floor -- Senator Jeff Merkley even held it for 15 hours -- to highlight the outrageousness of confirming Trump's nominee while he's under FBI investigation.

If Mitch McConnell hadn't attacked the filibuster, our Democratic resistance would have worked. But this isn't a normal moment. Republicans took an unprecedented step today and Senate Democrats need you now more than ever.

**Senate Democrats aren 't giving up -- and we can't either, friend. Chip in $1 to show Republicans we're coming for them in 2018:**
